title: Create second visual mode
summary: Implement a second geometry system (WaveField) alongside the existing ParticleField, plus a mode manager that auto-switches between them based on seed and elapsed time, without interrupting audio playback.

existing_architecture.geometry_contract: GeometrySystem interface in src/visual/types.ts requires init(ctx, seed, params) and draw(ctx, frameState)
existing_architecture.frame_state: FrameState provides elapsed time, delta, canvas dimensions, pointer position, and VisualParams (10 normalized scalars including bassEnergy, trebleEnergy, paletteHue, etc.)
existing_architecture.mode_manager: ModeManager in src/visual/modeManager.ts already wraps multiple ModeEntry objects and auto-switches on seeded intervals (90-180s)
existing_architecture.registration_point: main.ts passes mode entries to createModeManager()

analysis.key_insight: The GeometrySystem interface and ModeManager infrastructure are already in place. The second mode just needs to implement the same interface with a visually distinct rendering approach, then register in main.ts.

steps.title: Verify GeometrySystem interface and ModeManager contracts
steps.description: Read src/visual/types.ts and src/visual/modeManager.ts to confirm the exact interface contract (init/draw signatures, FrameState shape, ModeEntry structure) and switching behavior before implementing.

steps.title: Study ParticleField as reference implementation
steps.description: Read src/visual/systems/particleField.ts to understand how it uses seeded PRNG for init, maps VisualParams to rendering behavior, and handles pointer disturbance. This is the template for the new mode.

steps.title: Implement WaveField geometry system
steps.description: Create src/visual/systems/waveField.ts implementing GeometrySystem. The wave mode renders layered horizontal sine waves that respond to the same VisualParams. Key mappings: bassEnergy drives wave amplitude (macro motion), trebleEnergy drives line brightness/width (shimmer), cadence drives phase speed, density controls wave count, pointerDisturbance warps waves near cursor, paletteHue/Saturation set colors with per-wave hue offsets. Uses seeded PRNG for initial wave positions, frequencies, and phase offsets.

Wave.y: vertical position (stacked evenly across canvas height)

config.enableShimmer: default true, disable on low quality

visual_param_usage.bassEnergy: multiplies wave amplitude (1 + bassEnergy * 3) for macro motion
visual_param_usage.trebleEnergy: modulates line width and alpha for shimmer effect
visual_param_usage.motionAmplitude: master gate on all wave motion
visual_param_usage.pointerDisturbance: locally warps wave Y near pointer position
visual_param_usage.paletteHue: base HSL hue for wave stroke color
visual_param_usage.paletteSaturation: HSL saturation for wave stroke color
visual_param_usage.curveSoftness: controls bezier smoothness vs angular segments
visual_param_usage.structureComplexity: adds harmonic overtones to wave shapes

details.rendering_approach: Canvas 2D path drawing with beginPath/moveTo/lineTo or bezierCurveTo across canvas width, one stroke per wave, layered back-to-front with decreasing alpha

steps.title: Register WaveField in main.ts mode manager
steps.description: Add WaveField as a second ModeEntry in the createModeManager call in main.ts. Pass quality-tier-appropriate config (maxWaves, enableShimmer) matching the pattern used for ParticleField.

details.registration: Add { name: 'waves', factory: () => createWaveField({ maxWaves: quality.maxWaves ?? 20, enableShimmer: quality.enableSparkle }) } to the modes array
details.quality_tier: May need to add maxWaves to QualityTier if not already present, or derive from existing maxParticles

steps.title: Verify mode switching does not break audio playback
steps.description: Confirm that ModeManager.switchMode() only calls geometrySystem.init() on the new mode and does not touch audio deps. The render loop in renderLoop.ts should continue polling the analyser pipeline across mode transitions without interruption. Test by running dev server and observing a mode switch.

steps.title: Build and manual smoke test
steps.description: Run npm run build to verify no type errors or build failures. Run npm run dev and verify: (1) both modes render correctly, (2) mode switch occurs after 90-180s, (3) audio continues across switch, (4) mute toggle still works, (5) info overlay still works, (6) no console errors, (7) no forbidden storage APIs.

acceptance_criteria_check.same_seed_and_audio: Both modes receive identical FrameState with same seed, same VisualParams (including bassEnergy/trebleEnergy from shared analyser)
acceptance_criteria_check.mode_choice_seed_or_time: ModeManager selects initial mode via seeded PRNG, switches based on elapsed time (90-180s seeded interval)
acceptance_criteria_check.no_playback_break: Audio pipeline is decoupled from geometry; mode switch only reinits canvas drawing

risks.risk: Wave rendering performance on low-end devices
risks.mitigation: Cap maxWaves based on quality tier; use simple lineTo segments instead of bezier curves on low tier; skip shimmer effects when enableShimmer is false
ModeManager already handles clean init/re-init. Could add a brief fade-to-black transition (optional, not required by acceptance criteria)

notes: The architecture is well-prepared for additional modes. The GeometrySystem interface is minimal (init + draw), the ModeManager handles switching logic, and all inputs flow through the shared FrameState. Adding the WaveField is primarily a creative/rendering task, not an architectural one. The mode boundaries are clean by design — each system is a standalone module with no cross-dependencies.
